page: Microsoft Azure E64-16as_v4 is an EASv4-series virtual machine configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz, the system features 8 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The underlying architecture is x86_64, supported by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It includes 885 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not feature any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the middle 50%. Memory performance is a key highlight, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25%. However, a notable tradeoff is the large block memory write bandwidth, which is categorized in the bottom 25% at 22.75 GB/sec, while read-only memory bandwidth remains average.

Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32 GB per vCPU, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads. It is suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and memory-heavy enterprise applications. The qualitative cost profile reflects a high-density memory allocation relative to compute resources, making it cost-efficient for workloads requiring massive RAM footprints without needing proportional scaling of CPU cores.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-16as_v4 is an EASv4-series virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor operating at 3.525 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance includes 885 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance and a strong PassMark memory score, both placing in the top 25%. Conversely, large block memory write bandwidth is a relative bottleneck, performing in the bottom 25%. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32 GB per vCPU, this configuration is optimized for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and data-heavy enterprise applications where RAM capacity is prioritized over raw multi-core compute scaling.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16as_v4 is an EASv4 virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and 885 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16as_v4 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 885 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio, dedicated AMD EPYC processing, and local storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz
512 GB of system memory providing a high 32 GB per vCPU ratio
885 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core CPU and memory performance benchmarks
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-heavy enterprise applications
